subject: ap-precalculus
title: Logarithmic Regression: Soil Temperature & Depth
stimulus:
A geologist measures soil temperature (in degrees Celsius) at various depths below the surface and records the following data:

| Depth $d$ (meters) | Temperature $T$ ($^\circ$C) |
|---|---|
| 1 | 12.0 |
| 4 | 18.4 |
| 9 | 22.6 |
| 16 | 26.0 |
| 25 | 28.7 |

The geologist proposes the model $T(d) = a + b\ln(d)$ to represent soil temperature as a function of depth.

parts.prompt:
(i) Use the data at $d = 1$ and $d = 25$ to write two equations that can be used to find the values of constants $a$ and $b$.

(ii) Find the values of $a$ and $b$ as decimal approximations. Show the computations that lead to your answer.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Two correct equations — $a + b\ln(1) = 12.0$, which simplifies to $a = 12.0$; and $a + b\ln(25) = 28.7$; equivalently, $12 + b\ln(25) = 28.7$

1 pt: Correct values $a = 12.0$ and $b = \frac{28.7 - 12.0}{\ln(25)} = \frac{16.7}{3.2189} \approx 5.189$ (accept values in $[5.18, 5.20]$); supporting work must show the substitution and division step
parts.scoring_notes: The first equation immediately gives $a = 12$ since $\ln(1) = 0$. The second equation then gives $b = 16.7 / \ln(25) \approx 5.189$. Decimal must be correct to three decimal places. Full credit for both values if shown correctly. Accept $T(d) = 12 + 5.189\ln(d)$ as a complete response.

parts.prompt:
(i) Use the model $T(d) = 12 + 5.189\ln(d)$ to predict the soil temperature at a depth of $d = 50$ meters. Express your answer as a decimal approximation.

(ii) A geologist at another site obtains data that, on a semi-log plot of $T$ versus $\ln(d)$, appears approximately linear with a positive slope. What does this indicate about the relationship between temperature and depth at that site? Explain your reasoning.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Correct prediction — $T(50) = 12 + 5.189\ln(50) = 12 + 5.189 \cdot 3.912 = 12 + 20.299 \approx 32.299$ degrees Celsius (accept values in $[32.2, 32.4]$); supporting work must show substitution

1 pt: Correct reasoning — a linear semi-log plot of $T$ vs. $\ln(d)$ indicates a logarithmic relationship $T = a + b\ln(d)$; a positive slope means $b > 0$, so temperature increases as depth increases; the plot being linear confirms the logarithmic model fits well
parts.scoring_notes: For part (i), $\ln(50) \approx 3.9120$, so $5.189 \times 3.9120 \approx 20.30$, giving $T \approx 32.30$°C. For part (ii), the key insight is that linearity on the semi-log ($T$ vs. $\ln d$) plot means the logarithmic model is appropriate, and positive slope means temperature increases with depth. A response that only says 'temperature increases with depth' without explaining the semi-log relationship earns only partial credit.

parts.prompt:
(i) The geologist considers an alternative exponential model of the form $T(d) = A \cdot e^{kd}$ to compete with the logarithmic model. She plots $\ln(T)$ versus $d$ (a semi-log plot) and plots $\ln(T)$ versus $\ln(d)$ (a log-log plot). Using the data at $d = 1$ and $d = 25$, compute the slope of the line on the semi-log plot ($\ln T$ versus $d$). Then explain, based on the slopes and linearity of the two plots, how the geologist can determine which model — exponential $T = Ae^{kd}$ or logarithmic $T = a + b\ln(d)$ — better fits the data.

(ii) Use the logarithmic model $T(d) = 12 + 5.189\ln(d)$ to solve the inequality $T(d) > 25$. Determine the range of depths $d$ for which the model predicts the soil temperature exceeds $25^\circ$C. Express your answer as a decimal approximation with supporting work.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Correct semi-log slope AND valid comparison — slope of $\ln T$ vs. $d$ plot: $\frac{\ln(28.7) - \ln(12.0)}{25 - 1} = \frac{3.356 - 2.485}{24} = \frac{0.871}{24} \approx 0.0363$ (accept $[0.035, 0.037]$); supporting work must show the quotient; explanation must state that if the semi-log plot ($\ln T$ vs. $d$) is more linear, the exponential model fits better; if the log-log plot ($\ln T$ vs. $\ln d$) is more linear, a power model fits better; but a direct plot of $T$ vs. $\ln d$ being linear (as in part b(ii)) indicates the logarithmic model $T = a + b\ln d$ is most appropriate — accept any reasoning that correctly identifies which plot's linearity supports which model

1 pt: Correct inequality solution — $12 + 5.189\ln(d) > 25 \Rightarrow 5.189\ln(d) > 13 \Rightarrow \ln(d) > \frac{13}{5.189} \approx 2.506 \Rightarrow d > e^{2.506} \approx 12.26$ meters (accept values in $[12.1, 12.4]$); the model predicts $T > 25^\circ$C for all depths $d > 12.26$ meters; supporting work must show the inequality direction is preserved (since $\ln$ is increasing, dividing by the positive coefficient $5.189$ preserves the inequality)
parts.scoring_notes: For part (i), the semi-log slope is $\approx 0.0363$. The key insight is: if the $T$ vs. $\ln d$ scatter plot is more linear than the $\ln T$ vs. $d$ scatter plot, the logarithmic model is the better fit. Students need not compute both slopes — they need to identify which plot supports which model and connect it to the structure of the models. Award the point for correct slope calculation plus any valid model-comparison reasoning. For part (ii), $13/5.189 \approx 2.506$, $e^{2.506} \approx 12.26$ m. The inequality direction must be preserved: since $5.189 > 0$, dividing preserves $>$; since $\ln$ is an increasing function, $\ln d > 2.506 \Leftrightarrow d > e^{2.506}$. Both steps (isolation of $\ln d$ and conversion to $d$) must appear.
